metadata.notes: Wingspan standard overhaul: removed legacy sections format, added rules_citations with key rulings, validated content against official rulebook.

subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Global Components

**Boards & References**
- 1 large game board — hex-grid map of Eastern Europa with territories, plus the Triumph Track (stars), Popularity Track, and Power Track along the edges
- 5 player mats (double-sided: Industrial, Engineering, Patriotic, Mechanical, Agricultural) — each arranges the same 4 action columns in a different layout
- 1 quick-reference guide summarizing all actions

**Card Decks**
- 42 combat cards (numbered 2–5, secret weapons for fights)
- 23 objective cards (private goals worth a star each)
- 28 encounter cards (story events with 3 choices)
- 12 Factory cards (powerful bonus actions acquired mid-game)

**Tokens & Currency**
- 80 resource tokens — 20 each of food (orange crates), metal (silver ingots), wood (brown logs), oil (black barrels)
- 2 cardboard power dials for secret combat bidding
- 80 cardboard coins in various denominations
- 12 encounter tokens (round discs placed on the map)
- 12 multiplier tokens for scoring
- 5 Riverwalk reference cards
- 5 quick-start cards for new players
- 6 structure bonus tiles (use 1 per game)

#### Per-Faction Components (x5 factions)

Each faction — Rusviet (red), Crimean (yellow), Nordic (blue), Polania (white), Saxony (black) — receives:

**Miniatures & Mat**
- 1 faction mat showing unique faction ability and 4 mech ability slots
- 1 character miniature
- 4 mech miniatures
- 8 small wooden workers

**Tracking Pieces**
- 6 star tokens (wooden stars)
- 4 structure tokens
- 4 recruit tokens (enlist cylinders)
- 6 technology cubes
- 1 action token, 1 popularity token, 1 power token

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Setting Up the Board
- Place the game board in the center of the table with the Triumph Track, Popularity Track, and Power Track visible along the edges
- Place 1 encounter token on each of the 11 territories with an encounter symbol printed on them
- Create a supply of resource tokens and coins near the board within reach of all players
- Shuffle the combat cards, encounter cards, and objective cards into 3 separate face-down decks near the board
- Randomly choose 1 of the 6 structure bonus tiles — place it face-up at the bottom of the Popularity Track; return the other 5 to the box
- Shuffle the Factory cards and place them face-down near the Factory hex in the center of the board — set out 1 more card than the number of players (e.g., 5 cards for 4 players); return extras to the box

#### Faction and Mat Assignment
- Randomly deal 1 faction mat and 1 player mat to each player — the combination creates each player's unique strategic puzzle
- Return any unused mats and extra Factory cards to the box
- Each player should now have 1 faction mat and 1 player mat, with the board showing encounter tokens placed, decks shuffled, and the structure bonus tile visible

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Faction Mat Setup
- **Power token** — Place on the Power Track at the starting value printed on your faction mat
- **Combat cards** — Draw the number shown on your faction mat; keep them hidden (opponents can see how many you have, but not their values)
- **Coins** — Take the number shown on your faction mat from the supply

#### Player Mat Setup
- **Popularity token** — Place on the Popularity Track at the starting value printed on your player mat
- **Objective cards** — Draw the number shown on your player mat; keep these secret
- **Technology cubes** — Place the 6 green cubes on the top-row action spaces that have small black square markers (these move to the bottom row when you Upgrade)
- **Structure tokens** — Place your 4 structures on the 4 structure boxes on the mat
- **Recruit tokens** — Place your 4 recruits on the 4 recruit spaces
- **Reserve workers** — Place your 6 reserve workers on the rectangular spaces in the Produce action area
- **Action token** — Set beside your player mat (used to mark which column you choose each turn)

#### Board Placement
- Place your character miniature on your faction's home base territory
- Place 2 workers on the 2 territories adjacent to your home base (your starting production workers)
- Keep your 4 mechs on the mech spaces of your faction mat — they deploy to the board during play

#### Starting the Game
- **Turn order** — The player with the lowest-numbered player mat goes first; play proceeds clockwise
- **Public information** — Track positions, units on the board, and each opponent's combat card count
- **Hidden information** — Combat card values and objective card content

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### What a Turn Looks Like
1. **Choose an action column** — Your player mat has 4 columns, each pairing a top-row action with a bottom-row action. Pick one column and place your action token on it.
2. **Repeat restriction** — You may NOT choose the same column you used on your previous turn. You must move your action token to a different column each turn.
3. **Resolve the top-row action (optional)** — Perform the top action of your chosen column: Move, Produce, Trade, or Bolster. You can skip it.
4. **Resolve the bottom-row action (optional)** — Perform the paired bottom action: Upgrade, Deploy, Build, or Enlist. Pay the resource cost shown. You can skip it.
5. **Star check** — If you completed an achievement (e.g., deploying all 4 mechs or winning combat), immediately place a star token on the Triumph Track.
6. **End your turn** — Pass play clockwise.

#### How the Game Ends
- **Immediate end** — The game ends instantly when any player places their 6th star on the Triumph Track, even mid-turn.
- **Common mistake** — Thinking you finish the round. You do not. The game stops the instant that 6th star lands.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Top-Row Actions
- **Move** — Move up to 2 of your units (characters, mechs, or workers), each moving 1 territory. Units can carry resource tokens along when they move.
- **Produce** — Choose up to 2 territories where you have workers. Each worker produces 1 resource matching the terrain: Farms produce food, Forests produce wood, Mountains produce metal, Tundra produces oil, Villages produce a new worker (from your player mat reserve).
- **Trade** — Pay 1 coin to gain 2 resources of your choice from the supply, placed on any territory you control. Alternatively, pay 1 coin to gain 1 popularity.
- **Bolster** — Gain power on the Power Track and/or draw combat cards, depending on what your player mat shows.

#### Bottom-Row Actions
- **Upgrade (costs oil)** — Move 1 technology cube from a top-row action space down to a bottom-row action space. This improves the top-row action's output AND reduces the bottom-row action's cost simultaneously.
- **Deploy (costs metal)** — Take 1 mech from your faction mat and place it on any territory where you have at least 1 worker. Deploying unlocks a faction-specific mech ability printed underneath it.
- **Build (costs wood)** — Take 1 structure from your player mat and place it on any territory where you have at least 1 worker. Structures give permanent effects and help control territory.
- **Enlist (costs food)** — Remove 1 recruit token from your player mat. You immediately gain a one-time bonus shown on the space, and permanently gain an ongoing bonus that triggers whenever you OR the player to your left or right takes that same bottom-row action.

#### Key Rules to Remember
- You choose ONE column per turn — the top and bottom action must come from the same column
- Both actions are optional — you can skip either or both
- Resources are spent from territories you control; you can combine resources across multiple territories to pay a cost
- **Common mistake** — Workers are NOT resources and cannot be spent as costs

subtopics.title: Combat
subtopics.content:
#### When Combat Happens
- **Trigger** — Combat occurs when your character or mech moves into a territory occupied by an opponent's character or mech
- **Workers alone do not trigger combat** — Only characters and mechs are combat units

#### How to Resolve Combat
1. **Set your power dial** — Both players simultaneously and secretly choose how much power to spend (0 up to your current power level)
2. **Add combat cards** — Each player may add up to 1 combat card per character or mech they have in the fight (e.g., character + 1 mech = up to 2 cards)
3. **Reveal** — Both players flip their dials. Add dial number plus any combat card values. Highest total wins. Ties go to the attacker.
4. **Spend the power** — Both players (winner AND loser) lose the power they bid from the Power Track

#### After Combat
- **Winner** — Stays on the territory and places a star on the Triumph Track (maximum 2 combat stars per player; Saxony has no limit)
- **Loser's units** — All retreat back to their home base. Resources on the territory stay behind for the winner.
- **Displaced workers** — If the loser had workers on the territory, those workers also retreat home. The loser draws 1 combat card per retreating worker. The winner loses 1 popularity per opponent worker displaced.
- **Common mistake** — Forgetting the popularity cost. Attacking a territory with 3 enemy workers costs 3 popularity, nearly a full scoring tier.

#### Combat Tips
- Combat card count is public information — use it to estimate opponent strength before committing
- You can never attack someone on their home base

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Encounters
- **Trigger** — When your character reaches a territory with an encounter token, pick up the token and draw an encounter card
- **Choose 1 of 3 options** — Each card tells a short story and offers 3 choices; pick the one that best suits your situation
- **Character only** — Only your character can resolve encounters, not mechs or workers
- Encounters are almost always worth a detour — the rewards are generous

#### The Factory
- **Location** — The large hex in the center of the board
- **Claiming a card** — The first time your character reaches the Factory, look at the face-up Factory cards and choose 1. This becomes a permanent 5th action column on your player mat.
- **Scoring value** — The Factory counts as 3 territories instead of 1 for whoever controls it at end-game
- **Limit** — Each player can only claim 1 Factory card per game

#### Structures
- **Mine** — Your character and mechs can use tunnel movement, traveling between any territories marked with tunnel symbols
- **Mill** — The territory with the Mill counts as an extra Produce territory for free (no worker needed there to produce)
- **Armory** — Whenever you take the Bolster action, gain +1 power beyond what your player mat shows
- **Monument** — Whenever you take the Bolster action, gain +1 popularity beyond what your player mat shows
- **Permanence** — Structures stay on the board for the entire game. You control any territory with your structure on it, even without units present, unless an opponent moves units onto that territory.

#### Stars (Achievements)
- Complete all 6 upgrades
- Deploy all 4 mechs
- Build all 4 structures
- Enlist all 4 recruits
- Place all 8 workers
- Complete 1 objective card
- Win combat (maximum 2 combat stars per player — Saxony has no limit)
- Reach 18 popularity
- Reach 16 power

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### When Does the Game End?
- The game ends immediately when any player places their 6th star on the Triumph Track — no finishing the round.

#### How to Calculate Your Score
- **Coins on hand** — All coins accumulated during the game
- **Stars, territories, and resources** — Your popularity level determines a multiplier:

| What You're Scoring | Tier 1 (0-6 popularity) | Tier 2 (7-12 popularity) | Tier 3 (13-18 popularity) |
|---|---|---|---|
| Each star you placed | 3 coins | 4 coins | 5 coins |
| Each territory you control | 2 coins | 3 coins | 4 coins |
| Every 2 resources you control | 1 coin | 2 coins | 3 coins |

- The difference between Tier 1 and Tier 3 can be 30+ coins.

#### Counting Territories
- Count every territory where you have at least 1 unit (worker, character, or mech) or an uncontested structure (no opponent units present)
- The Factory counts as 3 territories for whoever controls it
- **Common mistake** — Your home base does NOT count as a territory for scoring

#### Structure Bonus
- Score the randomly selected structure bonus tile from setup based on where you placed your structures on the board

#### Tiebreakers
1. **Most coins on hand**
2. **Most territories**
3. **Most resources**
4. **Most stars**

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Opening Priorities
- **Build a repeatable 2-3 turn loop.** You cannot pick the same column twice in a row, so plan which columns to alternate between. Your first 3 turns should flow naturally without forcing a dead turn (e.g., Produce turn 1, Move turn 2, Produce turn 3).
- **Upgrade early.** One early Upgrade improves a top-row action's output AND lowers a bottom-row cost simultaneously. If you can Upgrade on turn 1 or 2, do it.
- **Expand workers onto productive terrain.** More workers means more resources when you Produce, which means faster bottom-row completion and more stars. Push workers toward farms, forests, mountains, and tundra.
- **Protect your resources.** Resources sit on territories, not in your hand. If you leave 5 metal on an exposed frontier, an opponent can walk in and claim it all. Keep valuable stacks on defensible territories.
- **Track popularity from turn 1.** Popularity multiplies ALL three end-game scoring categories (stars, territories, resources). A great board at Tier 1 popularity loses to a decent board at Tier 3.
- **Use encounters for tempo.** Route your character toward encounter tokens early. Each encounter gives a free bonus from 3 options — a resource swing without spending an action.

#### Common Mistakes
- Picking a strong action now without planning what you'll do next turn (you cannot repeat the same column)
- Chasing extra combats after earning your 2 combat stars — those fights cost power and popularity for zero additional stars
- Leaving large resource stacks on undefended frontier territories where opponents can swoop in
- Forgetting about private objective cards until it is too late to pivot toward completing them
- Neglecting popularity — a strong board at Tier 1 scores far less than a modest board at Tier 3
- Rushing to place your 6th star while behind in territory, resources, and popularity conversion — triggering the end does not help if your score is lower

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Your First Game of Scythe

**Setup Decision**
- You receive a faction mat (unique abilities and starting position) and a player mat (action economy with 4 columns). The combination creates your unique strategic puzzle.
- Place your character on your home base hex
- Place 2 workers on the two territories next to your home base
- Set power, popularity, combat cards, and coins to the starting values on your mats

**Understanding Your Player Mat**
- Your player mat has 4 columns, each pairing a top-row action with a bottom-row action
- On your turn, pick ONE column — you can do the top action, the bottom action, or both
- **Key restriction** — You CANNOT pick the same column you used on your previous turn
- The green technology cubes on top-row actions move down to bottom-row actions when you Upgrade — each cube improves the top AND reduces the bottom cost

**Turns 1-3: Getting Your Engine Started**

*Turn 1*: Find a column with Produce on top. Produce resources with your 2 starting workers — each generates 1 resource matching its terrain. Take the bottom-row action too if affordable.

*Turn 2*: Switch to a DIFFERENT column. Good early options: Trade (pay 1 coin for 2 resources) or Bolster (gain power and combat cards). Take the bottom-row action if possible, especially Upgrade.

*Turn 3*: Another different column. Consider Moving your character toward an encounter token for free resources and bonuses.

**The Rhythm (Turns 4-10)**
- The 2-turn loop forms: Produce resources one turn, spend them on a bottom-row action the next
- Every bottom-row completion (Upgrade, Deploy, Build, Enlist) gets you closer to a star
- Deploy mechs for better mobility and combat readiness
- Build structures for permanent bonuses on key territories

**Mid-Game: Combat and Territory**
- Once you have mechs on the board, start contesting territories
- Before fighting, check your power level against your opponent's and count their combat cards
- Only fight with a clear advantage or when you need a combat star
- Each displaced enemy worker costs 1 popularity

**End Game: The Star Race**
- Watch everyone's star count — when someone places their 6th star, the game ends IMMEDIATELY
- Ensure your popularity is at Tier 2 or higher (7+)
- Control plenty of territories and stack resources for the final scoring multiplier

**First Game Target**
- Focus on building a smooth 2-turn loop
- Aim for 3-4 stars
- Get popularity to Tier 2 (7+)
- A score of 40-60 coins is solid for a first game

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Faction Strategy Guide

**Rusviet Union (Red) — Top Tier**
- **Unique ability** — You CAN repeat the same player mat column two turns in a row, breaking the core restriction
- **Strategy** — Find your strongest column and spam it relentlessly; your engine starts faster than everyone else's
- **Key mechs** — Township lets your character and mechs teleport to any village or the Factory. People's Army makes your workers add +1 power each in combat.

**Crimean Khanate (Yellow) — High Tier**
- **Unique ability** — Spend combat cards as if they were any resource type; you are never resource-starved as long as you Bolster
- **Opening plan** — Bolster heavily in your first few turns, then use combat cards to rush through bottom-row actions
- **Key mech** — Scout lets you steal 1 opponent combat card before combat begins

**Nordic Kingdoms (Blue) — Mid Tier**
- **Unique ability** — Your workers can swim across rivers without needing the Riverwalk ability, giving the fastest early map expansion
- **Opening plan** — Push workers across rivers immediately to claim productive territories with diverse terrain types
- **Key mechs** — Seaworthy lets you move across lakes. Artillery subtracts 2 from your opponent's combat power.

**Polania Republic (White) — Mid-High Tier**
- **Unique ability** — Choose 2 encounter options instead of just 1, making encounters incredibly powerful
- **Opening plan** — Rush your character toward encounter tokens as fast as possible
- **Key mech** — Camaraderie means no popularity loss when displacing opponent workers in combat, removing the biggest downside of aggressive play

**Saxony Empire (Black) — Variable Tier**
- **Unique ability** — No limit on combat and objective stars (every other faction caps at 2 combat stars)
- **Opening plan** — Expand and fight early; every combat win is a star with no cap
- **Risk** — If opponents avoid fighting you, aggression yields fewer stars than a balanced approach

#### Player Mat Combos
- **Rusviet + Industrial** — Widely considered the strongest combination. Fast engine plus column repetition is dominant.
- **Crimea + Patriotic** — Resource flexibility from combat cards combined with strong enlist bonuses creates a dominant mid-game.
- **Saxony + Mechanical** — Cheap mech deployment gets you into combat fast, and unlimited combat stars reward the aggression.

#### Combat Strategy
- **Information advantage** — Your opponent's combat card count is public, and their power level is on the track for all to see. Calculate before committing.
- **Spend the minimum to win** — Overspending power is wasteful. Bid just enough to beat what you think your opponent will commit.
- **The zero bluff** — Sometimes bidding 0 power is correct. If your opponent overspends, they waste power. You lose nothing, retreat home, and retreating workers give you free combat cards.
- **Watch the popularity cost** — Each displaced opponent worker costs 1 popularity. Attacking a territory with 3 enemy workers means losing 3 popularity, nearly a full scoring tier.

#### Star Rush vs Economic Play
- **Star Rush** — Place 6 stars as fast as possible to end the game while opponents are still building. Best for Saxony (unlimited combat stars) and Rusviet (repeat columns for speed).
- **Economic Play** — Focus on Tier 3 popularity, holding many territories, and stockpiling resources. Let scoring multipliers win the math. Best for Nordic (early expansion) and Polania (encounter bonuses).
- **Balanced (recommended)** — Aim for 4-5 stars at a good pace, Tier 2+ popularity, and 5+ territories.

#### Key Decision Points
1. **Turns 1-3: Loop design** — The 2-3 turn repeating pattern you establish here shapes your entire game. Get it wrong and you feel stuck all game.
2. **Turns 5-8: Factory timing** — Is it worth detouring your character to the Factory for a 5th action column, or should you stay local and keep producing? The Factory also counts as 3 territories for scoring.
3. **Mid-game: First combat** — Your first fight is the most impactful. Time it when you have a clear power advantage.
4. **Late game: End timing** — Before placing your 6th star, do the math. Would 1-2 more turns to improve popularity or territory control score more coins than ending now?

#### Player Count Adjustments
- **2 Players** — The map is huge; combat is easily avoidable. Focus on engine efficiency and completing objectives.
- **3 Players** — Well balanced. Each faction has room to breathe, but territories start mattering for scoring.
- **4-5 Players** — The map gets tight and combat becomes frequent. Popularity management is critical because displacement penalties add up fast. Territory control drives final scores.

key_rulings.text: If the attacking player has the same total power as the defending player (including combat card values), the attacker wins.
You may not place your action token on the same section of your player mat as the previous turn. You must move your action token to a different section each turn.
Workers cannot move into a territory controlled by an opponent's workers, character, or mechs. Only characters and mechs can initiate combat by moving into an opponent-controlled territory.
At the end of the game, the Factory is worth a total of 3 territories (instead of 1) to the player who controls it.

key_rulings.topic: Home base is not a territory
key_rulings.text: Your home base is not a territory. It does not count for end-game scoring. You cannot move units into an opponent's home base.
If you win combat and your opponent has workers on the territory, each of those workers retreats to the opponent's home base, and you lose 1 popularity for each worker displaced.
Each player may place a maximum of 2 stars from winning combat. The Saxony Empire faction is the exception — they have no limit on the number of combat or objective stars they may place.
Resources are not owned by players. They are on specific territories. You control resources on territories where you have at least one unit (worker, character, or mech) or an uncontested structure.
If you produce on a territory with 1-2 workers, there is no additional cost. Producing on territories with 3-4 total workers costs 1 power. 5-6 workers costs 2 power. 7-8 workers costs 3 power.

A structure on a territory means you control that territory even without any units present — unless an opponent moves units onto that territory. Structures remain permanently on the board even if an opponent takes the territory.
